title: Codeium Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and techniques for using Codeium effectively in coding projects.

commands.description: Enables streaming with the GPT-4o model for real-time code suggestions.
commands.scenario: When you need continuous code suggestions while typing in an IDE.

commands.description: Sets the model's temperature for more deterministic outputs.
commands.scenario: When building a code review bot that needs deterministic output.

commands.description: Limits the maximum number of tokens in a generated response.
commands.scenario: When you need concise code snippets without excessive verbosity.

commands.description: Prompts the model to provide a detailed explanation of code.
commands.scenario: When you need to understand complex code logic for debugging.

commands.description: Specifies the programming language for more accurate code suggestions.
commands.scenario: When working on a Python project and need language-specific assistance.

commands.command: "Generate test cases for the following function."
commands.description: Asks the model to create test cases for a given function.
commands.scenario: When you need to ensure code reliability by testing edge cases.

commands.description: Sets the context length for the model to consider in its responses.
commands.scenario: When dealing with large codebases and need broader context for suggestions.

commands.description: Instructs the model to optimize code for better performance.
commands.scenario: When you need to enhance the efficiency of existing code.

commands.description: Sets the API key for authenticating requests to the Codeium API.
commands.scenario: When integrating Codeium into your development workflow programmatically.

commands.description: Prompts the model to add comments to a function for better understanding.
commands.scenario: When you need to improve code readability and maintainability.

commands.description: Sets a timeout for API requests to prevent long waits.
commands.scenario: When you need to ensure quick responses from the API during development.

commands.command: "What are the potential security vulnerabilities in this code?"
commands.description: Asks the model to identify security issues in the code.
commands.scenario: When performing a security audit on a codebase.

commands.description: Sets the logging level to debug for detailed logs.
commands.scenario: When troubleshooting issues with Codeium's integration.

commands.description: Requests a plain English summary of the given code.
commands.scenario: When you need a high-level overview of what a piece of code does.

commands.description: Limit the context window to specific file patterns to prevent token overflow and improve relevance.

commands.command: Act as a Senior Site Reliability Engineer. Review this code for race conditions and deadlocks.
commands.description: Role-based prompting to shift the model's persona toward specific architectural concerns like concurrency.

commands.command: --system-prompt "You are a strict TypeScript expert. Use only interfaces, no types."
commands.description: Enforce strict coding standards and stylistic constraints at the system level.

commands.command: Convert this logic into a functional programming paradigm using map/reduce/filter.
commands.description: Request specific architectural or stylistic transformations beyond simple refactoring.
Set temperature to absolute zero for deterministic, repeatable code generation in production pipelines.
